RG-SAN consists of two main components: the Text-driven Localization Module (TLM) and the Rule-guided Weak Supervision (RWS) strategy.

The Text-driven Localization Module (TLM) and the Rule-guided Weak Supervision (RWS) strategy are the two main components mentioned in the context. TLM focuses on locating all mentioned instances and refining their positions iteratively for continuous improvement in location accuracy. On the other hand, RWS utilizes dependency tree rules to precisely guide the positioning of core instances.
The Rule-guided Weak Supervision (RWS) strategy leverages dependency tree rules to precisely guide the positioning of core instances.
The Text-driven Localization Module (TLM) ensures continuous improvement in location accuracy by iteratively refining the positions of all mentioned instances.
The Rule-guided Weak Supervision (RWS) strategy significantly improves the handling of spatial ambiguities in referring expressions by precisely guiding the positioning of core instances.
